edges.description: Reduced anorexigenic signaling weakens meal termination and promotes persistent hunger.
Combined hyperphagia and neuroendocrine dysregulation produce persistent positive energy balance.
edges.target: Reduced energy expenditure and rapid adipose accumulation

nodes.description: Reduced activation of hypothalamic anorexigenic circuits produces persistent hunger, impaired meal termination, and food-seeking behavior. This is a prominent clinical consequence across acquired hypothalamic obesity, PWS, and the major monogenic leptin-melanocortin disorders.

evidence.reference_title: Acquired hypothalamic obesity: A clinical overview and update.
evidence.snippet: Individuals with hyperphagia are perpetually hungry. They do not experience fullness at the end of a meal, nor do they feel satiated after meals, leading them to consume larger and more frequent meals.

evidence.reference_title: Hypothalamic neuropeptides and neurocircuitries in Prader Willi syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Hypothalamic dysfunction in controlling body weight and food intake is a hallmark of PWS.

evidence.reference_title: Obesity Due to Steroid Receptor Coactivator-1 Deficiency Is Associated With Endocrine and Metabolic Abnormalities.
evidence.snippet: SRC-1 variant carriers exhibit hyperphagia in childhood, severe obesity, and clinical features of partial hormone resistance.
Biallelic pathogenic leptin gene variants cause severe early-onset obesity usually associated with low or undetectable circulating leptin levels.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and molecular genetic spectrum of congenital deficiency of the leptin receptor.
evidence.snippet: Congenital leptin-receptor deficiency should be considered in the differential diagnosis in any child with hyperphagia and severe obesity in the absence of developmental delay or dysmorphism.

evidence.reference_title: Evaluation of a melanocortin-4 receptor (MC4R) agonist (Setmelanotide) in MC4R deficiency.
evidence.snippet: While POMC deficiency is very rare, 1-5% of severely obese individuals harbor heterozygous mutations in MC4R.

nodes.description: The core shared mechanism is impaired signaling through the hypothalamic leptin-melanocortin pathway. In acquired hypothalamic obesity this follows injury to melanocortin-responsive nuclei and circuits. In monogenic forms it follows defects in leptin, leptin receptor, POMC-derived ligands, PCSK1, MC4R itself, or intracellular pathway regulators such as SH2B1 and NCOA1. In syndromic obesity, upstream defects such as ciliary leptin resistance in Bardet-Biedl syndrome or hypothalamic dysfunction in Prader-Willi syndrome converge on the same satiety pathway.

biological_processes: adenylate cyclase-modulating G protein-coupled receptor signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: Acquired hypothalamic obesity: A clinical overview and update.
evidence.snippet: Genetic forms of HO, including the monogenic obesity syndromes, often impact central leptin-melanocortin pathways.

evidence.reference_title: Natural History of Obesity Due to POMC, PCSK1, and LEPR Deficiency and the Impact of Setmelanotide.
evidence.snippet: Rare homozygous or biallelic variants in POMC, PCSK1, and LEPR can disrupt signaling through the melanocortin-4 receptor (MC4R) pathway, resulting in hyperphagia and severe early-onset obesity.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular basis of the obesity associated with Bardet-Biedl syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Indeed, BBS proteins have emerged as important mediators of leptin receptor trafficking, and loss of BBS genes results in leptin resistance that could be due to abnormal leptin receptor handling in a subset of leptin-responsive neurons.

evidence.reference_title: Obesity Due to Steroid Receptor Coactivator-1 Deficiency Is Associated With Endocrine and Metabolic Abnormalities.
evidence.snippet: Genetic variants affecting the nuclear hormone receptor coactivator steroid receptor coactivator, SRC-1, have been identified in people with severe obesity and impair melanocortin signaling in cells and mice.

evidence.reference_title: Natural History of Obesity Due to POMC, PCSK1, and LEPR Deficiency and the Impact of Setmelanotide.
evidence.snippet: Rare homozygous or biallelic variants in POMC, PCSK1, and LEPR can disrupt signaling through the melanocortin-4 receptor (MC4R) pathway, resulting in hyperphagia and severe early-onset obesity.

evidence.reference_title: Efficacy and safety of setmelanotide, an MC4R agonist, in individuals with severe obesity due to LEPR or POMC deficiency: single-arm, open-label, multicentre, phase 3 trials.
evidence.snippet: We report the results from trials of the MC4R agonist setmelanotide in individuals with severe obesity due to either pro-opiomelanocortin (POMC) deficiency obesity or leptin receptor (LEPR) deficiency obesity.

nodes.description: Pathway failure affects both food intake and energy expenditure. In acquired hypothalamic obesity, structural injury can also reduce sympathetic output and promote leptin and insulin resistance. Congenital structural hypothalamic abnormalities show a similar directional association between greater hypothalamic abnormality and higher body mass index.
nodes.id: Reduced energy expenditure and rapid adipose accumulation

evidence.reference_title: Acquired hypothalamic obesity: A clinical overview and update.
evidence.snippet: Structural damage in these hypothalamic nuclei often leads to hyperphagia, central insulin and leptin resistance, decreased sympathetic activity, low energy expenditure, and increased energy storage in adipose tissue, the collective effect of which is rapid weight gain.

evidence.reference_title: Body mass index and hypothalamic morphology on MRI in children with congenital midline cerebral abnormalities.
evidence.snippet: We conclude that there was a trend of increasing mean BMI SDS with increasing hypothalamic abnormality, although this was not statistically significant.

evidence.reference_title: Human SH2B1 mutations are associated with maladaptive behaviors and obesity.
evidence.snippet: We identified SH2B1 loss-of-function mutations in a large cohort of patients with severe early-onset obesity.
